input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Grounded Low Voltage/GLV`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Grounded Low Voltage/GLV` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.1.2
            TITLE: grounded low voltage - glv

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.1.2: Grounded Low Voltage - GLV
EV.1.2 Grounded Low Voltage - GLV 
Every electrical part that is not part of the Tractive System

RULE: ev.4.4.4
            TITLE: low voltage batteries must meet t.9.2

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.4.4: Low Voltage Batteries must meet T.9.2
EV.4.4.4 Low Voltage Batteries must meet T.9.2
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 92 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.9.1.2
            TITLE: low voltage - lv

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.1.2: Low Voltage - LV
T.9.1.2 Low Voltage - LV  
Any voltage less than and including 60 V DC or 25 V AC RMS

RULE: ev.7.9.2
            TITLE: the grounded low voltage master switch (glvms) must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.9.2: The Grounded Low Voltage Master Switch (GLVMS) must:
EV.7.9.2 The Grounded Low Voltage Master Switch (GLVMS) must: 
a. Completely stop all power to the GLV System  EV.4.4 
b. Be in the center of a completely red circular area of > 50 mm in diameter 
c. Be labeled “LV”

RULE: ev.4.4.1
            TITLE: the glv system must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.4.1: The GLV System must be:
EV.4.4.1 The GLV System must be: 
a. A Low Voltage system that is Grounded to the Chassis 
b. Able to operate with Accumulator removed from the vehicle

RULE: ev.9.2
            TITLE: low voltage (glv) system

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.2: Low Voltage (GLV) System
EV.9.2 Low Voltage (GLV) System 
The Shutdown Circuit may be Closed when or after the GLV System is energized  EV.7.2.4

RULE: ic.8.3.1
            TITLE: voltage between any two electrical connections must be low voltage t.9.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.8.3.1: Voltage between any two electrical connections must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2
IC.8.3.1 Voltage between any two electrical connections must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2

RULE: ev.6.6.1
            TITLE: all electrical systems (both low voltage and high voltage) must have appropriate overcurrent

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.6.1: All electrical systems (both Low Voltage and High Voltage) must have appropriate Overcurrent
EV.6.6.1 All electrical systems (both Low Voltage and High Voltage) must have appropriate Overcurrent 
Protection/Fusing.

RULE: t.9.2.1
            TITLE: all low voltage batteries and onboard power supplies must be securely mounted inside the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.2.1: All Low Voltage Batteries and onboard power supplies must be securely mounted inside the
T.9.2.1 All Low Voltage Batteries and onboard power supplies must be securely mounted inside the 
Chassis below the height of the Shoulder Belt Mount T.2.6

RULE: ev.6.6.5
            TITLE: battery packs with low voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.6.5: Battery packs with Low Voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be
EV.6.6.5 Battery packs with Low Voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be 
used when all three conditions are met:   
• An Overcurrent Protection device rated at less than or equal to one third the sum of the 
parallel fusible links and complying with EV.6.6.2.b above is connected in series.  
• The AMS can detect an open fusible link and will Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 if a 
fault is detected. 
• Fusible link current rating is specified in manufacturer’s data or suitable test data is 
provided.

RULE: t.9.2.2
            TITLE: all low voltage batteries must have overcurrent protection that trips at or below the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.2.2: All Low Voltage batteries must have Overcurrent Protection that trips at or below the
T.9.2.2 All Low Voltage batteries must have Overcurrent Protection that trips at or below the 
maximum specified discharge current of the cells

RULE: ic.8.2
            TITLE: batteries

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.8.2: Batteries
IC.8.2 Batteries  
Refer to T.9.2 for specific requirements of Low Voltage batteries

RULE: ev.9.1
            TITLE: activation sequence

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.1: Activation Sequence
EV.9.1 Activation Sequence 
The vehicle systems must energize in the following sequence: 
a. Low Voltage (GLV) System  EV.9.2 
b. Tractive System Active  EV.9.3 
c. Ready to Drive  EV.9.4

RULE: ev.6.6.6
            TITLE: if conductor ampacity is reduced below the ampacity of the upstream overcurrent protection,

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.6.6: If conductor ampacity is reduced below the ampacity of the upstream Overcurrent Protection,
EV.6.6.6 If conductor ampacity is reduced below the ampacity of the upstream Overcurrent Protection, 
the reduced conductor longer than 150 mm must have additional Overcurrent Protection.   
This additional Overcurrent Protection must be: 
a. 150 mm or less from the source end of the reduced conductor 
b. On both positive and negative conductors in the Tractive System 
c. On the positive conductor in the Grounded Low Voltage System

RULE: ev.6.6.7
            TITLE: cells with internal overcurrent protection may be used without external overcurrent

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.6.7: Cells with internal Overcurrent Protection may be used without external Overcurrent
EV.6.6.7 Cells with internal Overcurrent Protection may be used without external Overcurrent 
Protection if suitably rated. 
Most cell internal Overcurrent Protection devices are Low Voltage or non voltage rated and 
conditions of EV.6.6.5 above will apply.

RULE: t.9.1.1
            TITLE: high voltage – hv

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.1.1: High Voltage – HV
T.9.1.1 High Voltage – HV  
Any voltage more than 60 V DC or 25 V AC RMS

RULE: ev.6.7.2
            TITLE: grounded parts of the vehicle must have a resistance to glv system ground less than the

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.7.2: Grounded parts of the vehicle must have a resistance to GLV System Ground less than the
EV.6.7.2 Grounded parts of the vehicle must have a resistance to GLV System Ground less than the 
values specified below.   
a. Electrically conductive parts  300 mOhms (measured with a current of 1 A) 
Examples: parts made of steel, (anodized) aluminum, any other metal parts 
b. Parts which may become electrically conductive   5 Ohm 
Example:  carbon fiber parts 
Carbon fiber parts may need special measures such as using copper mesh or similar to 
keep the ground resistance below 5 Ohms.

RULE: ic.6.1
            TITLE: low pressure injection (lpi)

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.6.1: Low Pressure Injection (LPI)
IC.6.1 Low Pressure Injection (LPI) 
Low Pressure fuel injection systems are those functioning at a pressure below 10 Bar.  Most 
Port Fuel Injected (PFI) fuel systems are low pressure.

RULE: ev.5.5
            TITLE: high voltage disconnect - hvd

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5: High Voltage Disconnect - HVD
EV.5.5 High Voltage Disconnect - HVD 
A High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be included to quickly disconnect one or both poles of 
the Accumulator  EV.11.3.2

RULE: ev.5.5.1
            TITLE: the high voltage disconnect (hvd) must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.5.1: The High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be:
EV.5.5.1 The High Voltage Disconnect (HVD) must be: 
a. A directly accessible element, fuse or connector  
b. More than 350 mm from the ground 
c. Easily visible when standing behind the vehicle  
d. Operable in 10 seconds or less by an untrained person 
e. Operable without removing any bodywork or obstruction or using tools  
f. Directly operated.  Remote operation through a long handle, rope or wire is not 
acceptable.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 95 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
g. Clearly marked with "HVD"
